user_system_path: Master Audit #445 — Pass 9 (Vercel deploy/config). Forensic pass over vercel.json + next.config.mjs: security headers (strict CSP, X-Frame DENY, nosniff, frame-ancestors none, object-src none), single morning-pipeline cron (0 11 * * *), maxDuration set on all 4 pipeline routes (300/300/60/60), sensible marketing-alias redirects (/try,/signup,/request-access -> /start), correct HTML/session-JSON caching, Sentry wired. Header definitions cleanly split (security in vercel.json, cache/robots in next.config) — no duplication. Verdict PASS, no code change. Observations: O-9.1 legacy /try page + /api/try/analyze shadowed by redirects (routed to a guarded cleanup task, not deleted blind — e2e + unit tests exercise the boundary); O-9.2 CSP allows unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval (Next tradeoff, nonce hardening is future); O-9.3 maxDuration=300 assumes Pro tier (owner dashboard fact). Constraint: NO paid API calls.
allowed_files_raw: Pass 9 deploy/config record (docs/backend/VERCEL_DEPLOY_AUDIT.md) + control-plane only. Documentation pass; config verified healthy, no source change.
required_browser_proof: N/A — deploy-config audit; vercel.json + next.config.mjs read end-to-end, cron maxDuration confirmed on all 4 routes, header layering checked (no duplication).
acceptance_condition: Deploy config passes: strict CSP + security headers on all routes, single cron with maxDuration, no header duplication, sensible redirects/caching. Record in docs/backend/VERCEL_DEPLOY_AUDIT.md; /try dead-route cleanup routed to a guarded task.
anti_regression_checks: documentation-only; no runtime, schema, or UI change
no security-header regression — headers untouched, only audited
stop_condition: Stop when Pass 9 record is up as a PR and merged; continue to Pass 10 (GitHub CI).
next_command: Continue Master Audit #445: Pass 10 (GitHub CI), then 11 (observability), 12 (governance meta).
